english: (perh. the same as the following sbal-pa (sbal pa)), lág-pai (lag pa'i) the soft muscles of the inner hand, cf. also pyag-sbál (phyag sbal); the soft part of the paw of animals.

frog (rather scarce in Tibet), one Lex. कर्कट, crab, crawfish(?); sbál-pa dkár-po (sbal pa dkar po) Sty. stated to be a large species of frog; nágs-sbal (nags sbal) Lt. prob. tree-frog; rús-sbal (rus sbal) tortoise; sbal-čuṅ (sbal chung) or -lcoṅ (-lcong) Pth. 1. a young frog, tadpole Cs. 2. vulg. (from ignorance) lizard; sbal-rgyáb (sbal rgyab) S.g. tortoise-shell.
bud, eye, gem, sprout, shoot, ₒtoṅ ('tong) comes forth, ₒbye ('bye) opens Stg.
Ts. for sbúd-pa (sbud pa) bellows, instrument for blowing.
hollow, cavity, in the stem of a plant or a grass-blade Mil.
v. lbú-ba (lbu ba).
Ts. = bka-blon-sram (bka' blon sram) sable, mustela zibellina.
Ts. (*bu-hlán* (*bu hlan*)) plane, tool used in joinery.
Cs., *ₒbog-ₒčol* (*'bog 'col*) (?) Ld.-Glr.; *sbug-žál* (*sbug zhal*), *sbum-žól* (*sbum zhol*) W. large brass cymbal; *dúṅ-če* (*dung che*), *páb-če* (*pab che*) W. to play the cymbals.
= ₒbúgs-pa ('bugs pa), to perforate, to pierce.
Cs. hollow.

more frq. sbubs (sbubs), hollow, cavity, excavation, interior space, kùṅ-bui (kung bu'i) Lex. tubular cavity, in bones etc. S.g.; subterraneous passage, conduit, sewer C.; sbúbs-su ₒjúg-pa (sbubs su 'jug pa), sbúbs-nas ₒtón-pa (sbubs nas 'ton pa) to put into an underground hole or recess, to come forth from it Glr., Mil.; sbúg-tu nor sbá-ba (sbug tu nor sba ba) to hide money in such a place Lex.; hiding-place, hidden recess, = saṅ-seṅ (sang seng); hole for inserting the handle of some instrument Sch.; sảṅs kyi sbubs ynyis (sangs kyi sbubs gnyis) hollow, expanded nostrils Cs.; sbubs-ₒbyár (sbubs 'byar) Med. disease of the penis, prob. stoppage of its orifice by gonorrhoea, cf. mj̇e (mje).
(*bu-hág* (*bu hag*)) 1. the panting of a dog Sik. — 2. bassoon with a large and nearly globular bell-mouth W. —

1. vb. to light, kindle, set on fire, seldom, Lex.: mé-čas sbúd-pa (me chas sbud pa) q.v. — sbst. bellows, usually consisting of two skin-bags, the orifices of which are opened and shut by the hands, and which are then squeezed together, so that the compressed air passing through a tube is driven into the fire; sbúd-pa ₒbúd-pa (sbud pa 'bud pa) Cs. or rgyáṅ-ba (rgyang ba) Sch. to blow or work the bellows; sbud-rgyál (sbud rgyal) = sbúd-pa (sbud pa).
v. spún-pa (spun pa).
Pth. a small building in the style of a monument, in which sacred writings are deposited.
ant Cs., prob. identical with the following (cf. gróg-sbur (grog sbur)).
beetle, čú-sbur (chu sbur) S.g.; sbur-čén (sbur chen), -čuṅ (-chung), -dmár (-dmar), -mgyógs (-mgyogs) Cs., denoting various kinds of beetles.

= sbún-pa (sbun pa), chaff, husks etc.; rlúṅ-gis sbúr-ma bús-pa ltar (rlung gis sbur ma bus pa ltar) Dzl., sóg-sbur (sog sbur) čus yyéṅ-ba ltar (chus g.yeng ba ltar) Pth. like chaff scattered by the wind, carried along by the water; sbu-lén (sbu len) or -lóṅ (-long) amber Wts.
Lex. w.e.
Sch. to scuffle, wrestle.
lean, lank, thin S.g.

pf. sbas (sbas), fut. and common secondary form sba (sba), imp. sbos (sbos), W. *sbá-če* (*sba che*), pf. sbas (sbas), to hide, conceal, ytér (gter) a treasure, mdzód-du (mdzod du) in a store-house; má-mo sbéd-pai p̃ug (ma mo sbed pa'i phug) cavern in which a Mamo is concealed Mil.; dpúṅ-gi tsogs tsál-du (dpung gi tshogs tshal du) to conceal troops in a wood Dzl.; ytér-du (gter du) to deposit as a treasure Glr.; saí ₒóg-tu (sa'i 'og tu) in the ground Dzl.; also as much as to inter, to bury Dzl.; *sbás-te* (*sbas te*) or *bé-te bor-čé* (*be te bor che*) W. = sbéd-pa (sbed pa); *sbás-te* (*sbas te*) secretly, clandestinely, by stealth W.; mi sdíg-čan-la (mi sdig can la) lus sba pyir (lus sba phyir) in order to hide our form before sinful men, in order not to be recognized by them Mil.;
